text: At that time, is not in Greek. Capellus therefore argues in vain against the Greek author, as if this event took place in the 2d year. (Houbigant)<hr>The expression often occurs in Scripture, without determining the precise time. (Haydock)
When. Greek, "for he heard their deliberations."<hr>Told, by the mouth of Esther, Esther 2:21. (Haydock)

Palace, as an officer.<hr>Presents, of small value. (Calmet)<hr>The king had inquired, (Greek, Esther 6:3.) "What glory or favour have we done to Mardochai? and the ministers replied: Thou hast done nothing to him," to honour him as he deserves.

Bugite, may refer to some town of Macedon, Esther 3:1.<hr>Honour. Yet he might be still more exalted, after the conspiracy was detected; (Houbigant) as the king little suspected that he was concerned in it. (Haydock)<hr>Death. It is thought that they wished to place Aman, or some Macedonian, on the throne, Esther 16:12, 14. (Calmet)<hr>This reason for the malevolence of Aman, might be unknown to Mardochai, Esther 13:12. (Houbigant)<hr>The former was either a favourer of traitors, or perhaps of the same conspiracy. (Worthington)
